title: Carrier Sales — Post-Call Extraction
description: Fields the HappyRobot post-call extraction node should pull from the conversation. The agent's turns, the tool responses, and the carrier's words are all available as input. Emit null for anything not present.

mc_number.description: The MC number the carrier provided (digits only, no prefix). Null if they never gave one (e.g., abandoned before capture).

carrier_name.description: Legal carrier name from the verify_carrier tool response. Do not invent if verify_carrier wasn't called.

load_id.description: The load_id from search_loads that this call was about. Null for no_match, abandoned, or carrier_ineligible calls.

carrier_equipment.description: What equipment the carrier is running. One of: Dry Van, Reefer, Flatbed, Power Only, or null if not mentioned.

carrier_current_location.description: Where the carrier said they are right now (city, state). Null if they didn't mention it.

notes_from_carrier.description: Free-text summary of anything the carrier said that isn't standard: constraints (can't load before noon), preferences, concerns, asks. Keep under 200 chars.

counter_offers_made.description: Every USD number the carrier proposed during negotiation, in order. Empty array if they accepted the listed rate with no counter.

final_agreed_price.description: The USD number the call ultimately closed on. Present only when outcome=booked. Null for every other outcome.

negotiation_rounds.description: How many times the agent called evaluate_offer. 0 for instant accept at listed rate, or for calls that never entered negotiation (no_match, carrier_ineligible, abandoned).

example.notes_from_carrier: Can pick up first thing tomorrow morning
